Title: Successful user login
Preconditions:
- User has a valid account (registered via signup).
- Network connectivity is available.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters valid username (`john.doe`) and password (`SecurePass123!`).
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates credentials and redirects to the **Home** page.
- No error messages are displayed.
- User session is established (verified via subsequent requests).

Title: Failed login with incorrect credentials
Preconditions: - User has an account but is not currently logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters valid username (`john.doe`) and incorrect password (`WrongPass456`).
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error message: *"Invalid username or password."*
- User is **not** redirected to the Home page.
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Successful user registration
Preconditions:
- User is not registered.
- Network connectivity is available.
Test Data:
- Email: `jane.smith@example.com`
- Password: `NewPass789!`
- First Name: `Jane`
- Last Name: `Smith`
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters valid registration details (email, password, first/last name).
3. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates inputs and redirects to the **Home** page.
- Confirmation message: *"Registration successful. Please log in."*
- User account is created in the database (verified via login with new credentials).

Title: Failed registration with invalid email format
Test Data:
- Email: `jane.smith` (invalid format, missing `@` and domain)
- Password: `ValidPass123!`
- First Name: `Jane`
- Last Name: `Smith`
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters invalid email (`jane.smith`) and other valid details.
3. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: *"Please enter a valid email address."*
- User remains on the registration page.
- No account is created in the database.

Title: Registration with existing email
Preconditions: - User `existing.user@example.com` already exists in the system.
Test Data:
- Email: `existing.user@example.com` (duplicate)
- Password: `NewPass456!`
- First Name: `Duplicate`
- Last Name: `User`
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters an already registered email and other valid details.
3. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: *"Email already registered. Please log in."*
- User remains on the registration page.
- No new account is created.

Title: Emission calculation for registered user
Preconditions:
- User is logged in (authenticated).
- Emission calculation input fields are accessible.
Test Data:
- User inputs:
  - Vehicle type: `Sedan`
  - Distance traveled (km): `150`
  - Fuel type: `Gasoline`
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the emission calculation section (post-login).
2. User selects `Sedan` as vehicle type.
3. User enters `150` km as distance traveled.
4. User selects `Gasoline` as fuel type.
5. User submits the form.
Expected Result:
- System processes the input and displays calculated emissions (e.g., *"Your trip emitted 45.6 kg CO2."*).
- Calculation is logged in the `STATISTICHE` state (if applicable).
- No errors are displayed.

Title: Emission calculation with invalid distance (negative value)
Test Data:
- Vehicle type: `Sedan`
- Distance traveled: `-100` (invalid)
- Fuel type: `Gasoline`
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the emission calculation section.
2. User enters `-100` km as distance traveled.
3. User submits the form.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: *"Distance must be a positive number."*
- User remains on the calculation page.
- No emissions are calculated or logged.

Title: Emission calculation for unregistered user (access denied)
Test Steps: 1. User attempts to access the emission calculation section directly (e.g., via URL manipulation).
Expected Result:
- System redirects the user to the **Login** page with a message: *"Please log in to calculate emissions."*
- Emission calculation page is not accessible without authentication.

Title: End-to-end request flow (login → home → emission calculation)
Preconditions:
- User is not logged in.
- Network connectivity is available.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in with valid credentials (`test.user@example.com` / `TestPass123!`).
2. User is redirected to the **Home** page.
3. User navigates to the emission calculation section.
4. User enters `SUV`, `200 km`, and `Diesel`.
5. User submits the form.
Expected Result:
- Login succeeds; user reaches Home page.
- Emission calculation completes successfully (e.g., *"Your trip emitted 78.9 kg CO2."*).
- All interactions follow the sequence: **User → Application → Server → Database → Application → User**.
- No errors or redirects occur during the flow.

Title: Database connectivity failure during emission calculation
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Database server is temporarily unavailable (simulated).
Test Steps:
1. User submits emission calculation inputs.
2. System attempts to process the request but fails to connect to the database.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: *"Service unavailable. Please try again later."*
- User is not logged out.
- No emissions are calculated or logged.
- User can retry the calculation after connectivity is restored.

Title: View profile after successful login
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the "Profile" link in the menu.
Expected Result:
- System displays the user’s profile page with details (e.g., username, registration date).
- Profile data matches the registered account (e.g., `john.doe`, `john.doe@example.com`).
- No errors are displayed.

Title: Logout functionality
Expected Result:
- User is redirected to the login page.
- Session is terminated (verified by attempting to access a restricted page).
- Confirmation message: *"You have been logged out."*

Title: Access menu options post-login
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the "Menu" or "Orizzontale" (horizontal menu) option.
Expected Result:
- System displays the menu with available options (e.g., Home, Profile, Emissions, Logout).
- Menu options are consistent and functional (e.g., clicking "Home" redirects to the home page).

Title: Empty emission inputs
Test Data:
- Vehicle type: `<empty>`
- Distance traveled: `<empty>`
- Fuel type: `<empty>`
Test Steps: 1. User submits the emission calculation form with all fields empty.
Expected Result:
- System displays validation errors for each empty field (e.g., *"Vehicle type is required."*).
- User remains on the calculation page.
- No emissions are calculated.

Title: Maximum allowed distance input
Test Data: - Distance traveled: `999999` (exceeds reasonable limit, e.g., 10,000 km)
Test Steps:
1. User enters `999999` km as distance.
2. User submits the form.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: *"Distance must be ≤ 10,000 km."*
- User remains on the calculation page.
- No emissions are calculated.

Title: Case-sensitive login credentials
Test Steps:
1. User enters `JOHN.DOE@example.com` (uppercase) and correct password.
2. User clicks "Login."
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: *"Invalid username or password."*
- User is not logged in.
- System treats email as case-sensitive (or normalizes it; clarify in requirements).
